mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) have been declining from cycle peaks, and a sustained multi-month downtrend in HOUST signals weakening residential investment and consumer demand that precedes broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations adjust downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1580K reflects continued decline from prior peak levels above 2000K
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST 6-month trend and 60-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative HOUST trends coincide with positive forward returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals a labor market inflection that historically precedes risk-off equity positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in slowing consumption.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.2% as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of FEDFUNDS relative to CPIAUCSL-implied inflation determines the real policy rate; when nominal FEDFUNDS fails to keep pace with rising CPIAUCSL, implying deeply negative or compressing real rates, equity valuations are supported and S&P 500 forward returns over the next month are positive, whereas a rising real rate spread compresses valuations negatively.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL real rate proxy shows no significant relationship (Rank IC < 0.03) with 20-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window, or direction of real rate change is uncorrelated with return sign in more than 55% of periods.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle housing downturn regime, a joint decline in HOUST and stagnation or deceleration in RSAFS signals broad-based consumer and construction sector weakness that amplifies recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter that are stronger than either signal alone would predict.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% YoY while RSAFS growth decelerates below prior 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1580K reflects ongoing housing correction as of 2006
failure_condition: Joint HOUST-RSAFS deceleration signal fails to precede negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window restricted to housing downturn regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals healthy supply-side output and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as short-term momentum traders extrapolate strong production data into equity positioning.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.18 as of latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO month-over-month changes are not associated with positive 5-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below a 2% year-over-year threshold, it signals weakening consumer demand that precedes downward revisions to corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 568215 million as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Sub-2% RSAFS YoY growth periods are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint dynamic of decelerating payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) constitutes a compound labor-market deterioration signal analogous to a Sahm-rule trigger, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as recession probability repricing accelerates beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth turns negative while UNRATE 3-month moving average rises
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE rise fail to precede negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) has held at or above a restrictive level for an extended period without cuts, tightening financial conditions increasingly weigh on near-term equity sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week once the rate crosses a sustained restrictive threshold.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS held above 5% for 6+ consecutive months with no rate cuts
failure_condition: Sustained restrictive FEDFUNDS periods are followed by positive 5-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
